summary: 2026-06-25 10:16:39 PDT — Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/api-log-redaction.md`. The branch is scoped to the expected files and leaves the server log sink untouched, but I found two issues in the source-minimization/docs guarantees. I attempted `npm test`; it could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write `client/node_modules/.vite-temp`.

findings.title: Allowlisted fields can still carry arbitrary nested payloads
findings.claim: `buildClientErrorPayload` allowlists top-level keys, but it copies any non-string value for an allowed key verbatim. A caller-supplied object or array under `message`, `stack`, `filename`, `lineno`, `colno`, etc. would still be serialized to `/api/log`; because the spec deliberately leaves the server logging verbatim, this leaves a path for participant PII to ride through an allowed key and weakens the prevent-at-source invariant.
findings.suggestion: Normalize by expected type before assigning: cap/coerce string fields, keep only finite numbers for `lineno`/`colno`, and drop arrays/objects/functions rather than copying them.

findings.title: Privacy note overstates the telemetry guarantee
findings.claim: The new public policy says operational telemetry “never includes participant names or individual values,” but the implementation intentionally preserves runtime error text such as `message`, `stack`, and `componentStack` in `client/src/lib/reportError.js`. The spec accepts the residual risk that thrown exception text may contain a value, so this absolute public claim is not accurate for `client-error` telemetry.
findings.suggestion: Qualify the note: diagnostics record only shapes/no values, while error reports are minimized to error text plus fixed technical fields and do not include arbitrary participant payloads.
